# Japan in Seasons

> Live Japan seasonal travel data: cherry blossom (sakura) forecasts, autumn leaves (koyo), festivals, flowers, fruit picking, and short-range weather. 1,700+ GPS-tagged spots across all 47 prefectures. Sourced from Japan Meteorological Corporation, Japan Meteorological Agency, Open-Meteo, and official tourism boards. Free, open-source (MIT), with a Model Context Protocol server for AI assistants.

Built by Haoming Koo. MIT licensed. Project site: https://seasons.kooexperience.com

## Primary experiences
- [Cherry blossom forecast](https://seasons.kooexperience.com/): 48 observation cities with JMC bloom and full-bloom dates, historical averages, and 1,012+ sakura viewing spots across all 47 prefectures
- [Autumn leaves (koyo) forecast](https://seasons.kooexperience.com/): Koyo peak timing by city and region, with spot-level viewing locations
- [Festivals (matsuri, fireworks, winter)](https://seasons.kooexperience.com/): 46 curated recurring events with official URLs, typical dates, and GPS
- [Flowers](https://seasons.kooexperience.com/): Non-sakura blooms — plum, wisteria, hydrangea, lavender, sunflower, cosmos
- [Fruit picking](https://seasons.kooexperience.com/): Seasonal fruit guide plus a farm directory with GPS and booking links

## MCP server (for AI assistants)
- MCP endpoint: https://seasons.kooexperience.com/mcp
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/japan-seasons-mcp
- GitHub: https://github.com/haomingkoo/japan-seasons-mcp
- Tools: sakura_forecast, sakura_best_dates, sakura_spots, kawazu_forecast, koyo_forecast, koyo_best_dates, koyo_spots, flowers_spots, festivals_list, fruit_seasons, fruit_farms, weather_forecast

## Data sources
- Japan Meteorological Corporation (JMC) — official sakura and koyo bloom forecasts
- Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) — short-range weather per city
- Open-Meteo — spot-level weather by latitude/longitude
- Official tourism boards and festival organizer websites

## Key facts
- 48 sakura observation cities with JMC-scale bloom rate (0–100%) and full-bloom rate (0–100%)
- Full-bloom rate 90–100% indicates mankai (peak bloom)
- Best sakura viewing window: typically around the full-bloom date
- Best koyo viewing window: typically around each spot's peak
- All forecasts refresh daily at 09:00 JST
